title: Canonical Health Event
description: Neutral intermediate representation for the Progressive Harmonization ETL framework. Each event represents one atomic observation, measurement, or health-related data point, regardless of source.

event_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this canonical event (UUID v4).

subject_id.description: Pseudonymized subject identifier. Mapped from source-specific user IDs during ingestion.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp marking the start or occurrence time of the event. For point-in-time measurements, this is the measurement time. For periods, this is the period start.

timestamp_end.description: ISO 8601 timestamp marking the end of a period. Null for point-in-time events.

duration_seconds.description: Duration in seconds. Provided when the source supplies duration directly or derivable from timestamp/timestamp_end. Null for instantaneous events.

type.description: Broad event classification. 'measurement' for quantitative device readings. 'observation' for qualitative or contextual data. 'survey' for questionnaire responses. 'event' for discrete occurrences. 'summary' for aggregated/derived values. 'session' for data spanning a defined activity period (e.g., sleep session).

category.description: Source-derived category describing what is being measured. Uses the source's own terminology without semantic interpretation. Examples: 'steps', 'heart-rate', 'sleep', 'calories'. Not a controlled vocabulary -- standardization happens in the mapping stage.

granularity.description: Temporal granularity of this event. 'instant' for point-in-time. 'interval' for fixed-width time buckets (e.g., 15-min intraday). 'daily' for day-level summaries. 'session' for variable-length activity periods. 'unknown' when granularity cannot be determined.

payload.description: The measurement data. After decomposition, most events have a scalar value. Complex/composite events use raw_value for structured data.

value.description: The primary scalar value of this event after extraction. Numeric for most measurements (steps=258, heart_rate=72). String for categorical data. Null for composite events where meaning is in raw_value or components.

raw_value.description: The original value as extracted from the source for this specific event. For atomic events this may equal 'value' (as its original type). For composite events, this contains the structured sub-object. Preserves original types (string '3475' stays string).

unit.description: Unit of measurement. Null when the source does not specify units. Populated by the adapter when units are implicit (e.g., Fitbit steps -> 'count', distance -> 'km'). Not yet standardized -- UCUM normalization happens in the cleaning stage.

label.description: Human-readable label for the measurement as provided by or derived from the source. Examples: 'Daily Step Count', 'Resting Heart Rate'. Used for display and as input to semantic mapping.

components.description: For multi-component observations (e.g., a heart rate zone has name+min+max+minutes+calories). Each component is a named value. Null for simple scalar events.

unit.description: Unit for this component, if different from the parent event's unit.

context.description: Source and device metadata. Identifies where the data came from without semantic interpretation.

source.description: Identifier for the data source system (e.g., 'fitbit', 'withings', 'flexai').

device.description: Specific device model if known (e.g., 'Fitbit Charge 6', 'Withings Body+').

source_measurement_type.description: The original measurementType or data type identifier from the source, verbatim. For Fitbit: 'steps', 'heart-rate-intraday', 'sleep-log'. Preserves source terminology for debugging and reprocessing.

provenance.description: Traceability back to the original source record. Enables reprocessing and auditing without duplicating raw data in every event.

source_record_id.description: Identifier or hash of the original source record from which this event was derived. Used to look up the raw record in the raw data store.

group_id.description: Shared identifier among all canonical events derived from the same source record. Enables retrieval of sibling events. Null if the source record produces exactly one event.

parent_event_id.description: Reference to a parent canonical event. Used for intraday events pointing to their daily summary, or sleep stage events pointing to their sleep session. Null for top-level events.

ingested_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this event was created by the ingestion layer.

adapter.description: Identifier of the source adapter that produced this event (e.g., 'fitbit-charge6-v1'). Enables versioned reprocessing.

adapter_version.description: Semantic version of the adapter used (e.g., '1.0.0').

mapping.description: Semantic mapping to standard terminologies. Null/empty at ingestion, populated during the Mapping stage of the pipeline.

standard_code.description: Code in the target standard vocabulary (e.g., LOINC '55423-8' for steps, '8867-4' for heart rate).

standard_display.description: Human-readable display name from the standard vocabulary.

confidence.description: Confidence score for the mapping (0.0 to 1.0). 1.0 for deterministic rule-based mappings.

quality.description: Data quality tracking, populated progressively through cleaning, validation, and qualification stages.

flags.description: Quality flags accumulated during processing. Each flag identifies a specific quality characteristic or issue.

stage.description: Pipeline stage that produced this flag.

completeness.description: Completeness score (0.0 to 1.0) computed during validation.

plausibility.description: Plausibility assessment result from the Kahn framework validation.

stage.description: Current pipeline stage. Tracks where this event sits in the progressive harmonization pipeline.

extensions.description: Escape hatch for source-specific metadata that does not fit elsewhere. Keys should be namespaced by source (e.g., 'fitbit.datasetInterval', 'sleep.isMainSleep').
